description: Exposure as ionization in air (lesson fig rt1-3-2-fig-a): X/gamma source fires wavy photon rays through a glass-walled air cell between + (top) and − (bottom) plates; ion pairs form along the beam — electrons drift up, + ions drift down; plates and circuit light to an integrating meter with C/KG chip while the 1 KG DRY AIR bracket glows (charge per mass); amber R badge pulses by the mR/h dial for the field unit. No conversion text baked in — spec note lands at 8.0s.
